senses.definition: to a modest or partial extent that is not fully popular or widely liked
examples: The band remains semipopularly among the younger crowd.
The product sold semipopularly, not as a blockbuster hit.

senses.definition: partially or lightly porcelain-like in quality or appearance
examples: The teacup has a semiporcelain glaze, not pure porcelain.
She wore a semiporcelain hairpin with a faint sheen.

senses.definition: plural of semiporcelain; partially porcelain-like objects or items
examples: The cabinet held several semiporcelains from the old factory.
Collectors prized semiporcelains for their delicate glaze.

senses.definition: partly or somewhat pornographic in content
examples: The magazine carried semipornographic images not suitable for minors.
The film was criticized for its semipornographic scenes.

senses.definition: plural of semipornography; instances or genres of semipornographic material
examples: The library restricted access to semipornographies.
Collectors debated the existence of semipornographies in certain archives.

senses.definition: something that is partly pornographic in content
examples: The article discussed semipornography in early cinema.
Debates arose about the boundaries of semipornography.

senses.definition: partially porous; having small openings that allow some passage but not all
examples: The semiporous membrane lets certain molecules through.
A semiporous lid reduces spills while allowing airflow.

senses.definition: having a semiporphyritic texture or mineral composition (rare technical usage)
examples: The rock was described as semiporphyritic in the geologist's report.

senses.definition: partly portable; able to be moved with some effort but not fully portable
examples: The device is semportable and requires care when transporting.
It remains semportable due to its weight.

senses.definition: partially or limitedly related to a postal service or stamps; often used for stamps issued to raise funds
examples: The semipostal stamp supported charitable causes.
Collectors sought semipostal issues from the campaign.

senses.definition: plural of semipostal; stamps issued to raise funds
examples: The charity issued several semipostals over the year.
Collectors added semipostals to their collections.

senses.definition: partly practical or somewhat useful in practice
examples: The proposal seemed semipractical but still costly.
A semipractical solution could work in the short term.

senses.definition: describing materials (like some stones) that are valuable but not as valuable as precious ones
examples: Semi-precious stones include amethyst and topaz.
The necklace was set with semiprecious beads.

senses.definition: partial preservation; keeping something from deteriorating to some extent
examples: The archive underwent semipreservation to slow decay.
Semipreservation allowed access while maintaining integrity.

senses.definition: partially preserved; kept in a recoverable or altered state
examples: The semipreserved manuscript was fragile but legible.
A semipreserved cache of artifacts was discovered.

senses.definition: partly indigenous; having some but not full native characteristics
examples: The tribe described as semiprimigenous showed mixed heritage.
Semiprimigenous populations faced unique cultural challenges.

senses.definition: partly primitive or not fully developed in culture or technology
examples: The site showed semiprimitive tools and methods.
Semiprimitive techniques were used in early construction.

senses.definition: partial privacy; limited ability to keep information or life private
examples: In the open office, semiprivacy is hard to maintain.
The app offers semiprivacy features that protect some data.

senses.definition: partly reserved for private use or partially private in nature
examples: The semiprivate room was cheaper than a full private suite.
Semiprivate offices offer some discretion without full isolation.

senses.definition: a person who plays a sport at a level between amateur and professional
examples: He is a semipro basketball player who trains daily.
The league includes several semipro teams.

senses.definition: partly productive; yielding some results but not highly productive
examples: The method is semiproductive and needs refinement.
A semiproductive approach may work in learning contexts.

senses.definition: in a partially productive manner
examples: They worked semiproductively to meet the deadline.
Progress moved semiproductively this quarter.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being semiproductive
examples: The semiproductiveness of the team surprised managers.
They measured semiproductiveness to adjust processes.

senses.definition: the state or degree of being semiproductive
examples: Semiproductivity is common in exploratory projects.
Improving semiproductivity is a goal for the team.

senses.definition: partly profane or lacking sacredness; somewhat secular or irreverent
examples: The ceremony had semiprofane elements mixed with ritual.
Some listeners found the speech semiprofane in tone.
